tokens: And I'll pass you the LEC.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, the jett bag's gotten pretty dirty.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: This should be your portion (of the LEC).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Talking to himself, probably draping the LEC over the right (south) side of the ladder) Okay.
Going down on the Rover's side.
Ease on down the ladder here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, an extraordinary television picture here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: As I stand out here in the wonders of the unknown at Hadley, I sort of realize there's a fundamental truth to our nature.
(Pause) And this is exploration at its greatest.
(Long Pause as Dave moves away from the TV camera) Well, I see why we're in a tilt.
There's so much hummocky ground around here (that) we're on a slope of probably about 10 degrees.
And the left-rear foot pad is probably about 2 feet lower than the right-rear foot pad.
And the left-front's a little low too.
But the LM looks like it's in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Tell the Program Manager (Jim McDivitt) I guess I got his engine bell.
(Laughs) It's a little rise right under the center of the LM.
The rear leg's in a crater and the rim of the crater is right underneath the engine bell.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Sorry about that Jim, but IFR (Instrument Flight Rules) landings, you know.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, you can transfer the ETB.
I think you'll find the stability is pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Flicking the LEC again) Over the rail here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can look straight up and see our good Earth back there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ETB is on the ladder hook, and we'll pick the old MESA up here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And Jim, the (PLSS) diverter valve is yours - whatever position you'd like.
And did something else come out with the ETB?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The wrapping on the package for the LEC.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to come on out.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: One of the interesting things, Jim, is the momentum you generate.
Get going and...It's easy to get going, but once you get all that momentum going there, why, it takes a bit to stop.
(Pause) That looks like a reasonable place (that is, height) for the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) I think maybe a little higher.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, Dave, can you tell what I'm hung up on here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; let me come over (to the bottom of the ladder).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Coming up to take a look.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, and it's dirty out here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, I'm going to put a big circle around this glass ball, so we don't mess it up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want me to take it in the contingency sample?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, wish we had...Oh, we ought to document it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, that front pad is really loose, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; why don't you get my
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Gonna open this snap here; (we want to) take care of that little fellow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll get this glass ball here on the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, why don't you save it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Moving out along the LM shadow and out of the field-of-view) Okay.
I'm going to move out and get the contingency sample.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How do the PLSSs look now, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Reminds me of Sun Valley, (a ski resort in the mountains of southern Idaho).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think I can get a rock here.
It's about 2 inches, subrounded, in the contingency sample, along with the soil.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And did Dave get your EV visor down?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You might note for the next time around that, in addition to the Velcro on the MESA blankets, they have all the tape.
If we need tape, I guess we ought to learn how to do it all with tape on there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I have the contingency sample.
I'm taking it back to the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, that's really soft dirt there around the front footpad.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Like about 6 inches deep of soft material.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's also like Sun Valley, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, don't move back; I've got the tripod over here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Jim) That makes for easy trench digging.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let me get this out of your way first, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look at that little glass ball.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, I got to do my Fam now!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause as Dave starts to move the TV camera)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A crater here that I'm standing by, Joe, it's about a meter in diameter.
And then, there's a smaller crater right in the center of it, and that one has fragments around it that have glass exposed on them - where the larger crater does not have any glass exposed.
Just the smaller crater within the large one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And careful with the Sun, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, when I turn this thing back and point it at you at 12 o'clock, it's going to be looking right into the Sun, so you'd better think about that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Matter of fact, I think a little discretion here might put it over about 10:30 (southwest) or 11:00.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll tell you, looking even that way, with the Sun angle - Oop - why, by golly, it's pretty bright.
Joe, I'm going to swing the camera around towards the ground.
And now it's pointing back at the LM, but down.
I want you to take a look as I move it up slowly.
Make sure that we're okay on what you see.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we read all of that.
We're getting a beautiful picture now (of the ground and the TV cable coming out from the LM).
(Making a request) We're going to try to wind up with the tripod in the shade, if that's possible, looking back towards the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause, as Dave points the TV at the Rover to the right of the ladder) Managed to set it right in a crater.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Mag C is going on the 16 millimeter (movie camera).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That ought to do it for your TV, I hope.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want...You like it like that?
Or do you want to go to the settings in the checklist?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, I have the camera all configured for those (Rover deployment) pictures.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Eager to get going) Okay, Houston, I'll give you about 10 more seconds.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Very slightly more to the right so we can watch the Rover come down.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Responding to Joe) Okay, you want to leave those settings at f/8 instead of f/11?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's take a look at our Rover friend here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me see if I can get it under the pad so I don't trip on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause as Dave moves to the right side of the Rover)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm going to go up the platform (with the contingency sample).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pointing) How about this one over here, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, because I think it's loose.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Both walking hinges were open, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And...(I'll) take a look at the pins.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Contingency sample's on the platform, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I think they're...How does the pins look up there, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Pins look okay up here, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, the LRV tools should come down with that strap.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's just lay it (possibly the Rover tool) in there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) And I'll stick it right down here in case we need it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now, as you come down, don't disturb our little glass ball.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The Rover's going to come down into a slight tilt to the left.
But I think we'll be okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I want to get the camera, too, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It takes a while to unwind.
(Pause) Walking on all these slopes makes it sort of sporty, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're hooked up on the LEC, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Don't know whether I move it or not?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: TV still look okay to you, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, you're on the TV (cable), Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, why don't you just go around?
(Pause) You're on the TV with your left foot.
Your left foot's on the TV (cable).
No, you're still on it, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I've got to get around that (glass ball)...You would put that circle right there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause as the Rover starts to rotate down) Okay.
Looks like you're going to have to do the bulk of the work today.
(Pause) Up to about...(The forward wheels pop out) Easy, Jim!
Take it easy; take it easy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, just pull Just stand there a little easy.
Just pull real easy, right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause as Dave lowers the Rover another 10 to 20 degrees) Okay, we're...Oh, shoot.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Houston, the walking hinges are unlocked again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: They're supposed to be unlocked now, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Once you see those things unlocked up there in the stowed position, it doesn't give you too good a feeling.
(Pause) Looks like she's coming down okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, can you pull it out a little bit, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Outrigger cables are...Well, the one over there's not...(Pause as Dave releases his hold on the right-hand tape) Okay, outrigger cables are loose.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Watch...Watch the rope, and watch the glass ball.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, outrigger cable is loose and off.
(Pause as Dave retrieves the left-hand tape from secondary ladder strut where he draped it earlier) Okay, let's come down with the left tape.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like it's loose to me!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you go put the...Come on over and we'll (garbled) (Chuckles) Man, this thing's nice and light.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to get you the tool.
Maybe you can reach it, Jim.
(Pause as Dave reaches over to the ETB and grabs the Rover tool with his left hand) Maybe I can reach it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Need you to get this hinge pin over here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get the...(Pause) Oh, shoot.
See my hinge pin on my side?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like it's almost all the way in.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How about putting the tip of the tool on it and pushing it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now, let's...Let's line this up a little straighter.
Let's pull the rear-end back towards me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, chassis hinge pins are good on my side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Now if I could get the telescoping rods off.
(Pause as Dave lifts the front of the Rover from the left side) Okay, let's...Jim?
I'm not sure the telescoping rods are disconnected.
Let's pick it up and move it back and turn it around.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, turn it what, your way?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me...Put it down right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And maybe take it forward a little bit, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (At the front of the Rover, examining the attachment hardware) Well, the pin's out.
The rods...The whole saddle up here is still on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think we can maybe lift the front end up, can't we?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me get in there and lift it up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me twist it this way to give you a little more room.
(Pause as Dave pulls the back of the Rover three or four feet farther to the left) Okay.
Oh, you'll never get in there with the PLSS, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...verify you pulled the saddle pin, please
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yes, the saddle pin has been pulled.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Joe, the situation is that both pins are out of the saddle, and it still seems to be connected to the frame of the LRV.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We copy, and we're working it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's finish setting up the Rover, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I remember a guy who once said "dirt dirt" and it is (sic; intended to say "is it") ever!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, pull the Rover as far out as you can away from the LM, and then pull on the front end, if you could.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, by that, we mean lift up on the front end.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Does that mean pull up...[Stops to listen to Joe] Yeah.
Lift up on the front end.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Get this stowed, so I don't lose the tool.
(Long Pause) Okay, let's try that, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, pull it out as far as we can?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Back as far as we can?
(Pause) That's about as far back as we are going to be able to get it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If you want to hold it there, I'll get in front of it...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: See how I can clear this...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now, your PLSS is hung up, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's turn it around now, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay, I've got my grip here, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Come to your left; don't walk back!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want to get a downhill run here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, back up a little bit now.
Just back up a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughing) I've been watching that all morning.
I just about fell on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Have you got your side of the (instrument) console unlocked?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Man, they've really done it with the Velcro.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, you almost have to pull against the shear-force of that to get the seat up.
I had to really...really tug at it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) It's awfully bouncy too, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get your seat belt out later, I reckon.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Mine's in the...Yeah, I might as well get it now.
(Long Pause as Jim goes back around to his side of the Rover, deploys his seatbelt and then heads for the MESA) Give a holler when you're ready to drive, Dave, I'll come out and take pictures.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, looks like the brake's on, so I'll see if I can't hop in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And buckle up for safety here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause as Dave attaches his seatbelt) Okay, safety belt's on.
(Pause) Oh, you sit up a lot higher than in one g, but that makes sense, does it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Circuit breakers - all except the Aux(illiary) and the Nav(igation system) - are coming closed.
Okay, I get readings on bus B.
(Long Pause) All the switches are off, by the way.
Okay, Houston, are you ready to copy some numbers?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Amps (being drawn), of course, are at zero.
Okay, volts: on number 1 I've got about 82, and number 2 is reading zero.
Okay and on the battery temperature, I'm reading 68...[correcting himself] about 78 and 80.
And the motor temps are off-scale low, of course.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And the only discrepancy so far...I don't have any volts on number 2.
(Pause) PWM Select is Both; Drive Enable, 2 in forward, PWM 1, (and) reverse, PWM 2.
And Houston, I'll stand by for any comments you might have on that (zero voltage) readout.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I know you've rechecked your circuit breakers there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The circuit breakers are all in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, just let me know before you drive.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, we're standing by for you to drive away and monitor the amp...The amps on battery 2, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, 15 Volt DC is going to Secondary.
Steering: Forward, Bus A; and Rear to Bus D.
Drive Power: forward to Drive Power, forward to Bus A
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and if battery 2 is out on us, we'll have no rear steering or no rear drive.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, Jim, you can probably tell me if I've got any rear steering.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But I don't have any front steering.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Under Joe) Joe, you sure about that battery bit?
But I don't have any front steering, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, while you're rolling there, requesting Forward Steering to Bus C, Bus Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Cycle the forward steering circuit breaker, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, I go to Bus Charlie and the circuit breaker is cycled.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here, Jim, I'm going to bring her around here and let's get on with it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, we're going to have a great time with all these hills and mounds.
(Pause) Okay, think you can handle it there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay, temps look about the same, Houston.
(Pause) Jim, soon as you get that
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, as I look back behind us, it almost looks like we landed in a...Another, oh, 10 meters aft and we'd have been in Surveyor Crater.
transcript.speaker: Scott
